name: vueuse-functions
description: Apply VueUse composables where appropriate to build concise, maintainable Vue.js / Nuxt features.
capabilities: Maps Vue.js requirements to appropriate VueUse composables
Applies correct invocation patterns (AUTO, EXTERNAL, EXPLICIT_ONLY)
Provides implementation guidance for state management, element interactions, and browser APIs
References detailed documentation for 200+ VueUse functions
Suggests composable-based solutions over custom code
author: antfu
securitySummary: All static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The scanner misidentified TypeScript code examples in markdown documentation as shell commands and legitimate API documentation as credential access. This is a legitimate Vue.js development guide containing VueUse composable documentation. No malicious code, prompt injection attempts, or actual security risks detected. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Building Vue.js features from scratch leads to complex, hard-to-maintain code. This skill maps requirements to VueUse composables, applying correct patterns to keep implementations concise, performant, and maintainable.
limitations: Requires Vue 3 or Nuxt 3 project to use suggested composables
Does not install VueUse - user must have it installed or accepts explicit request
External dependency functions only used if user already has the dependency installed
Does not provide runtime code execution or file modifications

useCases.title: State Management in Vue 3 Apps
useCases.description: Implement reactive state using VueUse composables like useLocalStorage, useSessionStorage, createGlobalState, and useStorage for persistent, syncable state across components.

useCases.title: Browser API Integration
useCases.description: Add browser feature access such as clipboard, geolocation, media devices, and storage using documented VueUse composables with proper permission handling.

useCases.title: Element Interaction Features
useCases.description: Create draggable elements, detect visibility, track mouse position, and handle resize observers using VueUse element composables for interactive UIs.

promptTemplates.title: Basic State Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement a counter component in Vue 3 that persists its value to localStorage. Use VueUse composables where appropriate to keep the code concise.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants a simple persisted counter

promptTemplates.title: Browser Feature Integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Vue 3 component that allows users to copy text to clipboard using the Clipboard API. Include error handling for browsers that do not support the API.

promptTemplates.title: Element Interaction
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a modal dialog that closes when clicking outside of it. Use VueUse to detect clicks outside the modal element.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating a modal with click-outside detection

promptTemplates.title: Complex Feature with Multiple Composables
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a dark mode toggle for a Nuxt 3 application. Use VueUse to persist user preference, detect system preference, and animate the transition.

bestPractices: Always check VueUse documentation for the most current API details before implementation, as composables may evolve across versions.
Prefer AUTO invocation composables for common patterns like state, elements, and browser APIs to reduce boilerplate.
Use EXTERNAL composables only when the required dependency is already installed, or explicitly request installation from the user.
antiPatterns: Avoid writing custom event listeners when useEventListener provides cross-browser compatible, properly cleaned-up handlers.
Do not manually synchronize localStorage with refs - use useStorage which handles reactivity and persistence automatically.
Avoid imperative DOM manipulation for common patterns; use element composables like useElementBounding instead.

faq.question: Does this skill install VueUse?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance on using VueUse composables. For AUTO invocation functions, it assumes VueUse is available. For EXTERNAL functions, it suggests installation only if truly needed.

faq.question: What Vue versions are supported?
faq.answer: This skill targets Vue 3 and Nuxt 3 projects. VueUse version 10+ requires Vue 3. For Vue 2 projects, use VueUse version 9.x.

faq.question: How do I use a composable not listed here?
faq.answer: This skill documents commonly used composables. For additional functions, consult the official VueUse documentation at vueuse.org. The same principles apply - check invocation type and follow reference examples.

faq.question: Can I use these composables in script setup?
faq.answer: Yes. All VueUse composables are designed for Composition API and work seamlessly with script setup. They return refs that can be used directly in templates without .value.

faq.question: How does this skill handle SSR (Server-Side Rendering)?
faq.answer: Some composables have SSR considerations. The documentation notes when Nuxt 3 has built-in alternatives (like useStorage vs Nitro useStorage). Always check the reference notes for SSR caveats.

faq.question: What happens if a browser does not support a feature?
faq.answer: Most VueUse composables return nullable refs or have options for fallback behavior. Check the specific composable documentation for graceful degradation patterns and feature detection.
